name: Developments in Literacy
mission: Developments in Literacy (DIL) delivers transformative education to underserved children in Pakistan—especially girls—by operating high-quality schools, empowering local educators, and innovating technology solutions to ensure that every child succeeds.
programs: E-Libraries (Read to Grow, Read to Know)
theoryOfChange: Developments in Literacy (DIL) has a published 'Our Approach' document on their website that details their strategic framework. This document explains how their synthesis of technologically driven learning, rigorous teacher training, tailored curricula, and project-based activities enables students to apply concepts and reach their potential. It outlines their two complementary school models (Community Schools and Government Schools) and specific initiatives such as Ed-Tech, Teaching to Teach, Localized Curricula, E-Libraries, and Success Beyond School, all aimed at delivering high-quality education and empowering students, especially girls. Additionally, DIL states that 'The key to the success of DIL's projects is a robust monitoring framework and complete financial transparency,' indicating a structured approach to tracking results. The 'TEAL' program page further illustrates how this specific program aligns quality content, teacher development, and technology 'From strategy to impact' to create measurable change.
scoreSummary: DEVELOPMENTS IN LITERACY INC shows exceptional impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.dil.org/donate (Source: https://www.dil.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: DIL holds a 100.0/100 score, a program expense ratio of 89.8%, 15.6 months of working capital, and fundraising efficiency of $0.04 per $1 raised.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and maintains a zakat policy for donors.
The organization reported total revenue of $2,785,991.
The organization provides detailed reporting on program service expenses.
DIL operates 214 schools and tracks student transitions from primary to secondary education.
DIL monitors Matric Board Exam pass rates and student dropout rates as key performance indicators.
DIL offers various donation options including Zakat-eligible giving methods.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: DIL earns a strong overall rating due to its perfect financial transparency score and high impact in the global education sector. The organization demonstrates high efficiency in converting donations into direct educational services.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing donor alignment strategies to better connect with specific niche philanthropic segments
Expanding data reporting on long-term vocational outcomes for graduates

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows medium donor alignment as it addresses the urgent global cause of education in underserved regions [1]. Furthermore, DIL is Zakat-eligible, providing a structured policy for donors to fulfill their religious obligations through educational support for the needy [2][9].
dimension_explanations.impact: DIL demonstrates high impact through a capacity-building model that maintains a low cost per beneficiary and high academic standards, including strong Matric Board Exam pass rates [6][8]. The organization's financial health is verified by a 100.100.0/100 from Charity Navigator, reflecting its commitment to accountability and program service expenses [1][5].

baseline_narrative.headline: Developments in Literacy (DIL) empowers underserved children in Pakistan through high-quality education and a robust network of over 200 schools.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 89.8%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach students [1]
High fundraising efficiency with costs limited to $0.04 per $1 raised [1]
Strong liquidity with 15.6 months of working capital available [1]
baseline_narrative.summary: founded in 1997, Developments in Literacy (DIL) operates 214 schools serving 89,433 students across Pakistan, with a primary focus on gender equality and female education [1][6]. The organization maintains exceptional financial health, reporting a total revenue of $2,785,991 and a perfect Charity Navigator score of 100.0/100 [1][3].

areas_for_improvement.context: While the organization is <cite id="15">zakat-eligible</cite>, providing more explicit documentation on <cite id="16">zakat allocation and eligible asnaf categories</cite> would benefit Muslim donors.

alignment.improvement: DIL should <cite id="16">clarify its zakat allocation policy</cite> to better serve donors seeking specific asnaf-compliant giving.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust DIL due to its <cite id="3">100.0/100 from Charity Navigator score</cite> and <cite id="9">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>. The organization has <cite id="14">89.8% independent board members</cite> and a long <cite id="11">29-year track record</cite>.

impact.explanation: DIL shows high impact with a <cite id="5">89.8% program expense ratio</cite> and a low <cite id="12">cost of $4.55 per beneficiary</cite>. They track <cite id="19">four distinct outcome metrics</cite> through Candid.

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking transition and dropout rates ensures that school attendance translates into meaningful life advancement.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With <cite id="1">89,433 students</cite> already served, DIL has the infrastructure to scale its ed-tech and teacher training programs.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="5">89.8% program expense ratio</cite> and spends only <cite id="6">$0.05 to raise every dollar</cite>.
DIL demonstrates high efficacy with <cite id="7">84% of students transitioning to higher education levels</cite> and <cite id="10">79% of graduates proceeding to vocational training</cite>.
DIL holds a <cite id="3">perfect 100.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="9">Platinum Seal from Candid</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: DIL's impact is rooted in its long-term commitment to Pakistan's education sector, having <cite id="11">operated since 1997</cite>. Their model is highly cost-effective, with an estimated <cite id="12">cost of $4.55 per beneficiary</cite>, which is considered exceptional for the global education sector. This efficiency is paired with strong financial stability, as the organization maintains <cite id="13">15.6 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, ensuring program continuity even during economic fluctuations. Furthermore, their governance is overseen by an <cite id="14">independent board of 14 members</cite>, ensuring objective oversight of their <cite id="1">network of over 200 schools</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1997, Developments in Literacy (DIL) has established itself as a cornerstone of educational reform in Pakistan, operating <cite id="1">214 schools serving 89,433 students</cite> across the country. The organization focuses on empowering underserved children, particularly girls, through a model that combines high-quality instruction with <cite id="2">localized curricula and teacher development</cite>. DIL maintains an <cite id="3">overall score of 100.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting a commitment to transparency and fiscal responsibility. Financially, the organization reported <cite id="4">total revenue of $2,440,822</cite> in 2023, with an exceptional <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 89.8%</cite>. This efficiency is further highlighted by their <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.04 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that donor contributions are maximized for direct impact. Beyond basic literacy, DIL tracks sophisticated outcomes, including an <cite id="7">84% transition rate from primary to middle and secondary school</cite> and a <cite id="8">student dropout rate of only 5%</cite>, significantly outperforming regional averages. Their work is supported by a <cite id="9">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of recognition for nonprofit disclosure.
